violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Several foods were observed cooling in plastic containers with tight fitting lids. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
A knife was observed in poor repair (chipped). PIC took knife out of service at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
The plumbing under the dish machine was in poor repair (active leak observed being caught by pan). New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Dish washer technician arrived at the time of inspection to adjust dish machine. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023

Various foods were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All foods were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023

ROP fish was observed thawing with the package in tact in the walk-in. All packages were observed less than 38 degrees. All packages were sliced open. Proper thawing procedures reviewed with PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023
The "cooks fridge" was observed at 47 degrees and unable to recover temperature in an efficient manner. All foods in the unit were observed above 41 degrees. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023
